reasoning: The document contains multiple major structural and content defects, including severe table truncation (11 of 12 rows missing in Section 9.1) and the total omission of the 'Five-Year Action Plan' table. While programmatic shingling recall is relatively high (0.856), the LLM fidelity check reveals significant data loss within tables and a breakdown of heading hierarchy (structural score 0.545) that programmatic tools failed to detect. Given the 3x cost of publishing a defective document, human review is required to restore missing data and fix the navigation structure.
